q: Electrical energy can be transformed into a wide variety of other useful forms of ___.
The transformation of electrical energy into ___ energy is very useful and important.
Electrical energy is provided by source of ___ such as batteries and generators.
A steady current will flow through the conductor if a steady ___ is maintained between its ends.
Batteries and generators are called ___ of electromotive force.
A battery converts ___ energy into electrical energy to provide electrical power.
In an electric generator, ___ energy is converted into electrical energy.
A source of electromotive force has a positive terminal and a ___ terminal.
Positive charges are sent from the ___ terminal to the positive terminal within the source.
The main function of the source is to send negative charges from the ___ terminal to the negative terminal within the source.
Electromotive force, abbreviated as ___, is the amount of energy provided by the battery per unit charge to move round the complete circuit.
Emf is measured in unit of ___ ($V$).
One volt is equivalent to ___ of electric charge ($J\ C^{-1}$).
Despite its name, electromotive force is not actually a ___.
Generally, a source of electromotive force has an ___ resistance.
The internal resistance of a battery depends on its size, chemical properties, ___, and temperature.
A resistor of resistance $r$ must be regarded as being connected in ___ to the battery.
The potential difference across the resistor is $V = ___$.
The total work done in moving a unit positive charge round the complete circuit is $IR + Ir$, which is the ___ $E$ of the battery.
The equation $I = \frac{E}{R+r}$ is called the ___ equation.
The potential difference between the terminals of a battery when it is connected to an external circuit is given by $V = ___$.
When a battery having an internal resistance is used in an electric circuit, the potential difference between the terminals of the battery is always ___ than its emf.
The potential difference across the terminals of a battery connected to an external circuit is called the ___ voltage.
Terminal voltage is also referred to as ___ voltage.
Emf is an ___ voltage.
Terminal voltage is a ___ voltage.
___ a battery means the chemical energy of the battery which has been used up is now supplied back by electrical energy from some external source.
In charging a battery, the potential difference between the terminals is equal to $___$.
When two or more electromotive sources are connected in series, the resultant emf is the ___ sum of the individual emfs.
In the ___ arrangement, the currents leaving the batteries are in the same direction.
For batteries in series aiding, the resultant emf is $E_1 + ___$.
The total internal resistance of batteries in series aiding is $___$.
Two batteries in series aiding can be regarded as a single battery having an emf of $___$ and internal resistance $(r_1 + r_2)$.
In the ___ arrangement, the emfs of the batteries are in opposition.
The resultant emf in series opposing is the ___ between the individual emfs.
In series opposing, if $E_1$ is greater than $E_2$, the resultant emf is $___$.
The total internal resistance of two batteries in series opposing is still $___$.
When two batteries having equal emfs and equal internal resistances are connected in parallel, the resultant emf is the emf $E$ of a ___ battery.
In parallel combination, the ___ terminals of the batteries are connected together.
The resultant internal resistance of two identical batteries in parallel is $___$.
The current flowing through each battery in a parallel combination of two identical batteries is $___$.
Otherwise than connecting same polarities in parallel, batteries may be ___.
Electrical energy is energy associated with the ___ through any part of circuit.
Electrical energy can be generated from photovoltaic ___.
Electrical energy can be stored in other forms for future application using ___ cells.
Electrical energy can be stored in batteries, capacitors and ___.
By the definition of potential difference, the work done in bringing a unit positive charge from A to B is ___.
The work done $W$ in bringing the amount of charge $Q$ from A to B is $W = ___$.
The work done $W$ by the battery in taking charge $Q$ from A to B is the ___ supplied by the battery.
Electrical energy is transformed into heat energy because electrons ___ with the atoms in the resistor.
When electrons collide with atoms in a resistor, the atoms acquire additional energy and therefore ___ energy is produced.
The electrical energy $W$ produced by the battery is $W = VIt = I^2Rt = ___$.
If an electric motor is connected, electrical energy will be transformed into ___ energy.
The practical unit of electrical energy is ___ (kWh).
One kilowatt hour is equal to $___\ J$.
Electrical power is the rate of ___ of electrical energy.
Electrical power $P$ is expressed as $P = VI = I^2R = ___$.
The unit of electrical power $P$ is the ___ (W).
One watt is equivalent to 1 ___ ($J\ s^{-1}$).
One unit of electricity is equal to $___$.
When electric current passes through a conductor, the electrons are accelerated due to the ___.
English physicist ___ in 1841 experimentally studied the heating effect of electric current.
Heat energy is produced in the resistor $R$ which is used as a ___.
The electrical energy $W$ supplied by the battery is transformed into heat energy $H$ in the resistor according to $W = ___$.
The constant $J$ is called Joule's ___ equivalent of heat.
The value of Joule's mechanical equivalent of heat is $___\ J\ cal^{-1}$.
Joule's law of electricity and heat is represented by the equation $H = ___$.
Heat produced in a resistor is directly proportional to the ___ of the current.
Heat produced in a resistor is directly proportional to the ___ of the conductor.
Heat produced in a resistor is directly proportional to the ___ taken by the current to pass through it.
Heat produced $H$ can also be written as $H = \frac{VIt}{J} = ___$.
Electric stoves, electric cookers, and electric irons change electrical energy into ___ energy.
An electric light bulb is made of a glass bulb consisting of a ___ filament.
Tungsten filament has a high melting point of approximately $___\ ^{\circ}C$.
A small amount of ___, an inert gas, is added to the light bulb after the air is removed.
At a temperature of $1800\ ^{\circ}C$ the tungsten filament emits ___ light.
The inert gas reduces ___ of the filament in a light bulb.
The bulbs used for illuminating airfields have electrical power of the order of $___\ W$.
In an electric iron, a ___ is wound around the nichrome wire.
Mica is a good ___ and can withstand very high temperatures.
The heating element in an electric iron is controlled by a ___ that switches the current on and off.
An electric iron has an electrical power of $___\ W$.
In an electric stove, a coil of ___ is placed in the groove of heat-resistant material.
An electric stove has an electrical power ranging from $___\ W$.
According to Table 10.1, a laptop computer has an electrical power of $___\ W$.
A ___ has an electrical power of $100-250\ W$.
Radio and television receivers have an electrical power of approximately $___\ W$.
A ___ is a thin short wire of tin-lead alloy used to prevent electrical appliances from damage.
The fuse wire is fitted in a ___ tube (cartridge) or on a porcelain block.
A $3\ A$ fuse is used for an appliance rated as $___\ W$ or less in a $220\ V$ circuit.
For appliances above $700\ W$, a $___\ A$ fuse should be fitted.
The current stops flowing and electrical appliance in the circuit is not damaged because the fuse ___ and breaks the circuit.
The voltage and the power of a simple light bulb are usually specified as $220\ V$, $___\ W$ etc.
